skills.name: architecture-rescue
skills.description: Repo-wide or subsystem-wide architecture hygiene pass that surfaces deepening opportunities, shallow modules, leaky seams, and ownership drift. Default mode is report-only: no bead creation or execution unless explicitly requested.

skills.name: bootstrap-project-context
skills.description: Use when starting work in an unfamiliar repository, resuming after lost context, or when the user asks to bootstrap repo understanding before implementation.

skills.name: brainstorming
skills.description: Use before feature work only when the design is still unclear — for shaping vague product intent, comparing possible feature shapes, and turning fuzzy requests into a documented, approved spec before exploring or planning begins. Do not use this when the feature intent is already clear and the remaining work is to lock implementation decisions; that belongs to pulse:exploring. Trigger phrases: design, brainstorm, think through, what should we build, help me think, explore options, compare directions, unclear feature shape, vague product request.

skills.name: compounding
skills.description: Use after completed Pulse work when the user asks to capture post-cycle machine-readable learnings for future planning and execution.

metadata.position: 8 of 9 — runs after reviewing, before next feature

skills.name: dev-note
skills.description: Use only when the user explicitly asks to save, note, record, or preserve one learning from the current conversation as a raw developer note.

skills.name: dev-note-distil
skills.description: Use when the user asks to distill accumulated dev-notes into reader-facing topic knowledge, rather than to capture a new note or update machine memory.

skills.name: dream
skills.description: Use when the user asks for a manual consolidation pass over runtime artifacts into machine-readable Pulse memory outside the post-cycle compounding flow.

skills.name: executing
skills.description: Use when implementing approved Pulse beads, either as a swarm worker or as a single worker when swarming is unavailable.

skills.name: exploring
skills.description: Use before implementation planning when feature intent is clear but implementation decisions are still unresolved, and the user needs those decisions locked first.

metadata.position: 2 of 9 — runs after using-pulse, before planning

skills.name: gitnexus
skills.description: Use when Pulse planning or discovery needs graph-based architecture context, execution-flow tracing, symbol relationships, or blast-radius analysis beyond plain file search.

skills.name: planning
skills.description: Use after pulse:exploring when locked decisions are ready and the user needs an approved phase plan plus current-phase preparation for validation.

skills.name: preflight
skills.description: Use when starting any Pulse workflow, resuming a Pulse session, or before planning or execution when tool readiness or onboarding state may block the flow.

metadata.position: 0 - runs before using-pulse or any execution-capable phase

skills.name: prompt-leverage
skills.description: Use when the user asks to improve, structure, or templatize a prompt before execution by Codex or another AI agent.

skills.name: refresh-project-docs
skills.description: Use when repository documentation is stale and the user asks to refresh docs to match the current codebase and workflows.

skills.name: reviewing
skills.description: Use after execution completes when the user needs final quality verification and release-readiness review before closeout.

skills.name: swarming
skills.description: Use after pulse:validating approves execution and swarm mode is recommended, when the current phase should be run by coordinated parallel workers.

skills.name: systematic-debug-fix
skills.description: Use when the user asks for root-cause-first debugging of bugs, test failures, flaky behavior, build issues, or integration defects with tracked verification and regression lock-down.

skills.name: using-pulse
skills.description: Use when bootstrapping or resuming work in a Pulse project after pulse:preflight, or when a request needs Pulse phase selection and mode-aware routing.

skills.name: validating
skills.description: Use after planning and before execution to verify phase and bead readiness and enforce explicit Gate 3 approval before any implementation starts.

skills.name: writing-pulse-skills
skills.description: Use when creating a new pulse skill, editing an existing pulse skill, or verifying a skill works under pressure before deploying. Use when you need an agent skill to be bulletproof against rationalization. Do NOT use for project-specific AGENTS.md conventions or one-off solutions.
